tips: Always authenticate the user via email or name+zip before any other action, even if the user provides a user ID.
After authentication, retrieve user details and all order details before proposing any actions to the user.
Before any database update (cancel, modify, return, exchange), list the exact action details and obtain explicit user confirmation ("yes") before proceeding.
Do not skip steps or combine multiple tool calls in a single response; make only one tool call at a time and wait for the result.
When a user requests multiple actions (e.g., cancel and return), handle each action sequentially, confirming each one separately.
Use the calculate tool to provide refund totals before processing returns, and confirm the amount with the user.
If a user requests an action that requires information not yet retrieved (e.g., product details for a modification), fetch that data before proposing changes.
Transfer to a human agent only when the request cannot be handled within policy scope, and always call transfer_to_human_agents before notifying the user.
